contents.value:
```toml
[table_name]
key1 = "value1"
key2 = 42
key3 = true
```
Tables (also known as hash tables or dictionaries) are collections of key/value pairs. They are defined by headers in square brackets on a line by themselves. All key/value pairs beneath a header belong to that table until the next header or end of file.

```toml
[[products]]
name = "Hammer"
sku = 738594937

[[products]]
name = "Nail"
sku = 284758393
```
An array of tables is defined using double brackets `[[name]]`. Each header with the same name creates a new element in the array. Equivalent to an array of objects in JSON.

```toml
key = "value"
bare_key = 42
"quoted key" = true
```
Key/value pairs are the primary building block of a TOML document. Keys are on the left of the equals sign, values on the right. Keys may be bare (alphanumeric, dash, underscore) or quoted.

```toml
fruit.apple.color = "red"
fruit.apple.taste.sweet = true
```
Dotted keys allow grouping properties using dots as separators. Each dot-separated part is a bare or quoted key. `fruit.apple.color` is equivalent to defining `[fruit.apple]` and setting `color`.

```toml
# This is a full-line comment
key = "value" # This is an end-of-line comment
```
Comments start with `#` and continue to the end of the line. They can appear on their own line or after a key/value pair. Comments cannot appear inside strings.

```toml
# Basic string (allows escapes)
basic = "Hello\nWorld"

# Literal string (no escapes)
literal = 'C:\Users\path'

# Multi-line basic
multi = """
Roses are red
Violets are blue"""

# Multi-line literal
multi_lit = '''
No escapes \here
'''
```
TOML has four types of strings: basic ("), literal ('), multi-line basic ("""), and multi-line literal ('''). Basic strings support escape sequences; literal strings do not.

```toml
decimal = 42
positive = +99
negative = -17
hex = 0xDEADBEEF
octal = 0o755
binary = 0b11010110
with_sep = 1_000_000
```
Integers are whole numbers. They support decimal, hexadecimal (0x), octal (0o), and binary (0b) representations. Underscores between digits are allowed for readability. 64-bit (signed long) range expected.

```toml
fractional = 3.14
exponent = 5e+22
both = 6.626e-34
with_sep = 224_617.445_991
infinity = inf
not_a_number = nan
```
Floats are IEEE 754 64-bit double-precision values. They can have a fractional part, exponent part (e/E), or both. Special values `inf`, `+inf`, `-inf`, `nan` are supported.

```toml
enabled = true
debug = false
```
Booleans are always lowercase: `true` or `false`. No other casing is permitted.

```toml
# Offset Date-Time (full RFC 3339)
odt1 = 1979-05-27T07:32:00Z
odt2 = 1979-05-27T00:32:00-07:00

# Local Date-Time (no timezone)
ldt = 1979-05-27T07:32:00

# Local Date
ld = 1979-05-27

# Local Time
lt = 07:32:00
```
TOML supports four date/time types: offset date-time (full instant), local date-time (no timezone), local date, and local time. All follow RFC 3339 formatting.

```toml
# Inline array
colors = ["red", "yellow", "green"]

# Multi-line array
addresses = [
  "192.168.1.1",
  "10.0.0.1",
]

# Mixed types
mixed = [1, "two", 3.0, true]

# Nested arrays
nested = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
```
Arrays are square brackets with comma-separated values. They can span multiple lines. A trailing comma is allowed. Arrays may contain mixed types and can be nested.

```toml
[package]
name = "my-crate"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "A useful library"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
authors = ["Jane Doe <jane@example.com>"]
repository = "https://github.com/user/repo"
rust-version = "1.70"
```
**Cargo.toml** — The `[package]` section defines Rust crate metadata including name, version, Rust edition, license, authors, and links.

```toml
[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []
full = ["feature-a", "feature-b", "dep:optional-dep"]
feature-a = []
feature-b = ["feature-a"]
```
**Cargo.toml** — The `[features]` section defines conditional compilation features. The `default` key lists features enabled by default. Use `dep:name` syntax to enable optional dependencies.

```toml
[workspace]
members = [
  "crates/core",
  "crates/cli",
  "crates/utils",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
```
**Cargo.toml** — The `[workspace]` section manages multiple related Rust packages. They share a common `Cargo.lock`, output directory, and can share dependency versions via `[workspace.dependencies]`.

```toml
[profile.release]
opt-level = 3
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
strip = true
panic = "abort"

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 0
debug = true
```
**Cargo.toml** — Profile sections customize compiler settings per build profile. `[profile.release]` is for optimized builds; `[profile.dev]` is the default for development. Key settings: opt-level, lto, debug, strip, panic.

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
```
**pyproject.toml** — The `[build-system]` table (PEP 517/518) declares what tools are needed to build the Python project. `requires` lists build dependencies and `build-backend` specifies the build backend entry point.

```toml
[project]
name = "my-project"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "A useful Python package"
requires-python = ">=3.9"
license = { text = "MIT" }
authors = [
  { name = "Jane Doe", email = "jane@example.com" },
]
dependencies = [
  "requests>=2.28",
  "click>=8.0",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://example.com"
Repository = "https://github.com/user/repo"
```
**pyproject.toml** — The `[project]` table (PEP 621) provides standardized Python project metadata. It replaces setup.py/setup.cfg with a declarative format.

```toml
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_classes = ["Test*"]
addopts = "-v --tb=short --strict-markers"
markers = [
  "slow: marks tests as slow",
  "integration: integration tests",
]
```
**pyproject.toml** — Pytest configuration via `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`. Replaces pytest.ini with testpaths, addopts, markers, filterwarnings, and more.

```toml
[tool.black]
line-length = 88
target-version = ["py311"]
include = '\.pyi?$'
extend-exclude = '''/(\n  dist|build\n)/'''
```
**pyproject.toml** — Black formatter configuration. Key options: line-length (default 88), target-version, include/exclude patterns, and skip-string-normalization.

```toml
[tool.ruff]
line-length = 88
target-version = "py311"

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "N", "W"]
ignore = ["E501"]
fixable = ["ALL"]

[tool.ruff.format]
quote-style = "double"
```
**pyproject.toml** — Ruff linter/formatter configuration. A fast Python linter supporting hundreds of rules from flake8, isort, pyupgrade, and more.

```toml
[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.11"
strict = true
warn_return_any = true
disallow_untyped_defs = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "tests.*"
disallow_untyped_defs = false
```
**pyproject.toml** — Mypy type checker configuration. Supports strict mode, per-module overrides, and fine-grained error control.

```toml
[tool.poetry]
name = "my-project"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A Python project"
authors = ["Jane Doe <jane@example.com>"]
readme = "README.md"

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.9"
requests = "^2.28"

[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
pytest = "^7.0"
```
**pyproject.toml** — Poetry project manager configuration. Manages dependencies, virtual environments, and publishing. Uses semantic versioning with caret (^) and tilde (~) constraints.

```toml
[server]
host = "0.0.0.0"
port = 8080
ssl = true
cert_file = "certs/server.crt"
key_file = "certs/server.key"
workers = 4
read_timeout = 30
write_timeout = 30
```
Common server configuration pattern. Defines network binding, TLS/SSL settings, worker counts, and timeout values.

```toml
[database]
url = "postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb"
pool_size = 10
max_idle = 5
timeout = 30
ssl_mode = "require"
```
Common database configuration pattern. Defines connection URL (or DSN), connection pool settings, timeouts, and SSL mode.

```toml
[logging]
level = "info"
file = "logs/app.log"
format = "json"
max_size = "100MB"
max_backups = 5
compress = true
```
Common logging configuration pattern. Defines log level, output destination, format, and rotation policies.

```toml
[auth]
secret = "your-jwt-secret-key"
token_expiry = 3600
refresh_expiry = 86400
issuer = "my-app"
algorithm = "HS256"
```
Common authentication configuration pattern. Defines JWT secret, token/refresh expiry durations, issuer, and signing algorithm.

```toml
key = "value"
```
Basic strings are surrounded by quotation marks ("). Any Unicode character may be used except those that must be escaped: quotation mark, backslash, and the control characters (U+0000 to U+001F, U+007F).

```toml
key = 'value'
```
Literal strings are surrounded by single quotes. What you see is what you get — there is no escaping. Because there is no escaping, there is no way to write a single quote inside a literal string.

```toml
key = """\nmulti-line content\n"""
```
Multi-line basic strings are surrounded on each side by three quotation marks and allow newlines. A newline immediately following the opening delimiter will be trimmed. All other content between the delimiters is interpreted as-is without modification.

```toml
key = '''\nmulti-line content\n'''
```
Multi-line literal strings are surrounded on each side by three single quotes and allow newlines. No escaping whatsoever is performed. A newline immediately following the opening delimiter will be trimmed.

```toml
key = 2024-01-15T10:30:00+00:00
```
An offset date-time represents a specific instant in time, specified as a full date, a T delimiter (or space), a full time, and a timezone offset. Conforms to RFC 3339.

```toml
key = 2024-01-15T10:30:00
```
A local date-time omits the timezone offset. It represents a date-time without relation to any specific timezone or offset. Cannot be converted to an instant in time without additional information.

```toml
key = 2024-01-15
```
A local date represents the date portion of an RFC 3339 formatted date-time without the time component. It represents a whole day without time or timezone.

```toml
key = 10:30:00
```
A local time represents the time portion of an RFC 3339 formatted date-time without the date or timezone. Millisecond precision is supported.

```toml
[package]
```
Defines Rust package metadata in Cargo.toml. Contains fields like name, version, edition, description, license, authors, repository, documentation, homepage, and readme.

```toml
[dependencies]
```
Specifies the runtime dependencies of a Rust crate in Cargo.toml. Each entry maps a crate name to a version requirement, which can be a simple string or a table with version, features, optional, git, path, etc.

```toml
[dev-dependencies]
```
Specifies dependencies only needed for development in Cargo.toml. These are used for compiling tests, examples, and benchmarks but are not propagated to dependent crates.

```toml
[build-dependencies]
```
Specifies dependencies needed by build scripts (build.rs) in Cargo.toml. These are compiled and made available to the build script before the main crate is compiled.

```toml
[features]
```
Defines conditional compilation features for a Rust crate in Cargo.toml. Features can enable optional dependencies or other features. A 'default' key specifies features enabled by default.

```toml
[workspace]
```
Defines a Cargo workspace for managing multiple related packages. Contains members (list of package paths), exclude, default-members, and resolver fields.

```toml
[lib]
```
Configures the library target in Cargo.toml. Fields include name, path, crate-type (lib, cdylib, staticlib, etc.), and doc/test/bench toggles.

```toml
[[bin]]
```
Defines a binary target in Cargo.toml. Each [[bin]] entry specifies a separate binary with name, path, and optional required-features.

```toml
[profile.release]
```
Customizes the release build profile in Cargo.toml. Common settings include opt-level, lto, codegen-units, panic, strip, and debug.

```toml
[profile.dev]
```
Customizes the development build profile in Cargo.toml. The default profile used by `cargo build` and `cargo run`. Common settings include opt-level and debug.

```toml
[build-system]
```
Specifies the build system for a Python project in pyproject.toml, as defined by PEP 517/518. Contains 'requires' (list of build dependencies) and 'build-backend' (the build backend module).

```toml
[project]
```
Declares core Python project metadata in pyproject.toml as defined by PEP 621. Fields include name, version, description, requires-python, license, authors, dependencies, classifiers, urls, scripts, and entry-points.

```toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
```
Defines optional dependency groups (extras) for a Python project. Users install them with `pip install package[extra]`. Each key maps a group name to a list of dependency specifiers.

```toml
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
```
Configures Pytest within pyproject.toml. Supports testpaths, python_files, python_classes, python_functions, addopts, markers, filterwarnings, and more.

```toml
[tool.black]
```
Configures the Black code formatter within pyproject.toml. Options include line-length, target-version, include/exclude patterns, and skip-string-normalization.

```toml
[tool.ruff]
```
Configures the Ruff linter within pyproject.toml. Options include line-length, target-version, select/ignore rules, fixable/unfixable rules, and per-file overrides.

```toml
[tool.mypy]
```
Configures the Mypy static type checker within pyproject.toml. Options include python_version, strict, warn_return_any, disallow_untyped_defs, and per-module overrides.

```toml
[tool.setuptools]
```
Configures setuptools within pyproject.toml for package discovery, package data, and dynamic versioning.

```toml
[tool.poetry]
```
Configures a Poetry-managed Python project within pyproject.toml. Includes name, version, description, authors, dependencies, dev-dependencies, scripts, and plugins.

```toml
[server]
```
Common server configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes host, port, TLS/SSL settings, timeouts, and worker counts.

```toml
[database]
```
Common database configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes connection URL, pool size, max idle connections, and timeout settings.

```toml
[logging]
```
Common logging configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes log level, output file path, format (json/text), and rotation settings.

```toml
[auth]
```
Common authentication configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes JWT secret key, token expiry duration, refresh token settings, and issuer.
